Football.title: ‘Pedri is finally at Hogwarts’ – Barcelona check in at ‘Harry Potter hotel’ ahead of Newcastle clash
Football.content:
THE Barcelona squad have arrived in England ahead of their Champions League clash with Newcastle – and it was a magical welcome.

Hansi Flick‘s side have travelled early after beating Athletic Bilbao on Saturday to go four points clear of rivals Real Madrid in LaLiga.





They are staying at Matfen Hall, a near-200-year old mansion just outside of Newcastle, and it has been compared to Hogwarts.

The listed building was first established way back in the early 1400s but got a complete rebuild in 1832.

Now it resembles something out of Harry Potter with Barca players making the connection upon check-in.

A video of the group walking through wooden arched doors includes midfielder Gavi saying: “It’s like Hogwarts.

“Are we in Harry Potter? It’s very nice.”

The young Spanish star hobbled through the corridor with a golden chandelier above his head, a grandfather clock on the wall, and old tapestries hanging from the wall.



Barca’s social media account also went big on the wizarding world overlap, writing: “Pedri is finally in Hogwarts.”

Another post of Lamine Yamal and Fermin Lopez strolling in reads: “Your Potions lesson is about to begin.”

BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK

The players flew out directly from Bilbao with Marcus Rashford seen getting off the jet.

Striker Robert Lewandowski and goalkeeper Wojciech Szczęsny were also caught on camera.



Rashford was glued to his phone with wired headphones in as he took the steps down from the cabin with a fancy white bag.

The Barca players were all decked out in orangey-pink tracksuit tops as Pau Cubarsi, Ferran Torres, Joao Cancelo, Ronald Araujo, were all picked up.

They will be enjoying a stay at the historic house situated 18 miles away from St James’ Park.



Eddie Howe‘s Newcastle welcome Champions League royalty on Tuesday night in the last-16 first leg tie.

The Toon are coming off the back of losing to Manchester City in the FA Cup fifth round at home on Saturday.

And the crowd will be hoping to make things much more uncomfortable than their luxury hotel can offer.

The golf and spa estate is a five-star stay with over 300 acres of space, 63 bedrooms, five separate dining areas, and an 18-hole course to play.

Barca players will train at St James’ before the game and relax back at the retreat.



RECOMMENDED STORIES

It offers plush gardens, massive halls and rooms that could be straight out of Harry Potter.

Howe will hope to put his opponents under a spell come full-time when it gets down to business.

Football.title: Jack Grealish walks with knee scooter after foot surgery as Everton star gives health update following surgery
Football.content:
JACK GREALISH vowed to be back “stronger than ever” after posting a series of snaps from the early weeks of his injury recovery.

The Everton loanee, 30, was forced to undergo season-ending surgery last month after suffering a foot issue.





Grealish‘s foot needed to be severely wrapped in the wake of the injury, while he has been forced to wear a moon boot.

But looking to stay positive in the wake of adversity, he wrote on Instagram: “4 weeks post op an working hard to be back better an stronger than ever.”

The photo dump included a snap of Grealish using a knee scooter to get around, as well as some of him doing rehab work in the gym.

He also wore his hair down for one of the pictures, while in another he relaxed in a sauna.

Grealish’s post received well wishes from several fellow footballers, including James Maddison, Nathan Ake, Oleksandr Zinchenko and Jake Livermore.

While plenty of fans also left supportive messages.

One wrote: “Speedy recovery Jack. We miss seeing you out on that pitch.”



And a second added: “Get well soon mate.”

BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK

Grealish suffered the stress fracture in his foot during a match against former club Aston Villa in January.



After going under the knife, he wrote: “Didn’t want the season to end like this but that’s football, gutted.

“Surgery done and now all focus on getting back fit. I know for sure I will come back fitter, stronger and better than before.”

He went on to continue: “The support I’ve had since coming to this unbelievable club has meant the world to me.

“The staff, my teammates and especially the fans have been incredible and I absolutely love representing this club.

“I’ll be backing the lads all the way and doing everything I can to get back as soon as possible.



“Thank you again for all the love, it means so much. UTFT.”

Grealish made 20 Prem appearances for Everton, scoring twice and laying on six assists.

He will still have one year remaining on his Manchester City contract this summer, although Everton are believed to possess an option to make his switch permanent for around £50million.

Football.title: Man Utd hero, 39, shines on debut for Kazakhstan club then instantly wins over fans with chant
Football.content:
A MANCHESTER UNITED hero is already a fans’ favourite at his latest club after shining on his debut in KAZAKHSTAN.

The 39-year-old played for the Old Trafford outfit for seven years between 2007 and 2014 during a highly successful spell in the club’s history.





During that seven-year stint at the Red Devils he won four Premier League crowns as well as tasting Champions League glory.

He scored 41 goals and 65 assists across 230 appearances, more than playing his part in the trophy success.

Already figured out who it is?

It is of course, Nani.



Now, after coming out of retirement to pull his boots back at Aktobe, he is making waves at the other side of the world.

The Portugal legend was brought on for the final 20 minutes of the thumping 4-1 win over Tobol and had an instant impact.

He had only been on the park THREE MINUTES when he was on hand to tee up his team-mate to slam home Aktobe’s fourth goal.

After the match, he then celebrated his maiden appearance by saluting the fans.



CASINO SPECIAL – BEST CASINO BONUSES FROM £10 DEPOSITS

A video shows him interacting with the home support as he performs a chant.

It starts with him shouting “Ziga” as they then reply.

He then breaks into full voice with the passionate fans joining in with their new hero as they chant “Ziga-zaga hey.”

Nani was sporting a huge grin before jumping away and clapping, then hugging a colleague.

The victory got Aktobe up and running in the new Kazakhstan top flight season with a win in front of their own support.



Next up is a trip to face Kairat Almaty – a name both Arsenal and Celtic fans will be very familiar with.

Football.title: Premier League icon has ‘no intention’ of renewing £300,000-a-week contract and ‘will become free agent this summer’
Football.content:
BERNARDO SILVA is reportedly set to become a free agent after informing Manchester City he has “no intention” of renewing his current deal.

The Premier League icon, 31, earns £300,000-a-week but is out of contract this summer.



City are said to be keen on extending Silva’s terms for at least one more year, keeping him at the club for a decade.



However, Marca claims the Portugal international has decided it’s time to leave.

The versatile midfielder, who has two goals in 38 outings this season, will apparently see out of the rest of the season under Pep Guardiola.

But Silva has “no intention” of committing to another contract in the North West.

And in that case, he is set to become a free agent on the market this summer.



Silva, who has won six titles during his nine years at City, has long been linked with a move to Barcelona.

The Spaniards are reportedly still keen on signing him.

But Silva’s wages could prove to be too much for cash-strapped Barca, even if they win LaLiga again.

BEST FREE BETS AND BETTING SIGN UP OFFERS



It’s reckoned another destination could be Saudi Arabia, where host of international pals including Cristiano Ronaldo and Ruben Neves ply their trade.

Silva would stand to make a significant increase on his current £300k-a-week deal.

But he could yet be swayed by an emotional return to boyhood club Benfica, where he started his career in 2002.

Should Silva leave City, he is sure to get a grand send-off after establishing himself as a key player under Guardiola.

City paid £43million to sign him from Monaco in 2017 after he helped the French side to a shock Ligue 1 title.



During his time at the Etihad, Silva won the Premier League six times, the FA Cup twice and the Carabao Cup four times.

Silva also led City to their first Champions League victory in 2023.

And he currently boasts 75 goals across 445 games in all competitions.

Football.title: Footballer rushed to hospital with horror suspected double leg break as team-mates break down in tears and 3 sent off
Football.content:
THIS is the horror moment a footballer suffered a suspected double leg break in a sickening incident.

The Ecuadorian top flight game descended into chaos when poor Jean Pierre Arroyo Vernaza was brutally scythed down.





The young attacker had his ankle snapped following a wild sliding challenge from Mushuc Runa’s Brian Negro.

The horrific incident took place on the hour mark with home side Independiente del Valle level at 1-1.

But Negro’s horror tackle added fury to the encounter as he jumped in to try and stop Vernaza from running through on goal.



Replays show Negro’s right knee on the floor, smashing into Vernaza’s standing ankle before the trailing left leg follows through and brings him down.

Vernaza’s ankle can be seen flopping at an awkward angle as he is sent flying in the air.

He was rushed to hospital after being treated on the pitch for more than 10 minutes with an ambulance allowed onto the field to help him.

He is said to have suffered a fractured tibia and fibula in his leg and screamed out in pain immediately after the tackle, waving his arms around and crying for help.

The referee showed Negro a straight red card within seconds only for a widescale protest from the visiting team to follow.



BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK

Independiente subs rushed onto the pitch to help Vernaza and confront Negro with one bibbed player shoving him into the ref.

As Vernaza, 22, lay on the grass, a brawl between both sets of players broke out – with three players ultimately being sent off.



Runa player Crisopher Angulo, who would be shown red for his role in the ensuing fight, put his hands to his head in disbelief after seeing Vernaza.

He consoled some of the Independiente players with an arm around the shoulder and a pat on the back and head.

Independiente’s Jordy Alcivar was also sent off after being involved in the scrap.

He was seen shoving Angulo as the pair grappled with each other in close contact.

Play did resume but continued on past the 100th minute with Matias Mijail Perello scoring twice to win the game for Independiente.



They had to come from behind after Elvis Velasco’s surprise early opener.

Vernaza was the unfortunate story of the day, though, with former Manchester United wideman Antonio Valencia drawn to send him a message online.

99-cap Ecuador international Valencia wrote on X: “Stay strong in these tough times, you have to come back even stronger.

“There is no other option, friend.”



Bizarrely, Valencia, 40, included a high resolution image of the dislodged ankle sticking out of Vernaza’s boot.

There has been an outpouring of support for Vernaza with Negro telling the media afterwards: “First, I apologize.

“It was an unfortunate play, in which I caught his ankle and what happened, happened.

“At first, I didn’t realize it, and when they were thrown from the bench, I realized it then.



“The truth is that as a person and as a footballer, I feel terrible.

“I wish him a speedy recovery, I put myself at your family’s disposal.”

Independiente President Franklin Tello added: “He’s already at the Santa Cecilia clinic.

“We’re waiting for the X-ray results, and they’ll probably operate on him right away.

“So we’ll all be there for each other like a family.



“You’re left in shock because the bone displacement was so clearly visible. It’s a very difficult moment.”

And Independiente team-mate Mateo Carabajal said: “The three points don’t matter; they were meant to bring joy to Vernaza.

“If we don’t look out for each other, it’s difficult.”



The club also released a statement, writing: “Independiente del Valle, following the unfortunate injury suffered by our player Jean Pierre Arroyo-Vernaza, deeply appreciates the enormous number of messages, gestures of affection, and concern expressed by fans, clubs, and the entire football community.

“This support and solidarity become a fundamental source of strength for Jean Pierre to face this moment with courage and return even stronger to the pitch.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

“From the club, we send him all our strength and wish him a swift and successful recovery.

“The entire Independiente del Valle team and family stand with him in these difficult hours.”

Football.title: Leeds 3 Norwich 0: Watch Sean Longstaff’s worldie as he helps fire Whites through in FA Cup
Football.content:
LEEDS remain in the hunt for FA Cup glory after dumping out Norwich City.

The Premier League outfit avoided any sort of fifth round upset – unlike fellow top flight rivals Fulham and Sunderland – as they got the job done in the first half at Elland Road.





Philippe Clement has made an impact since joining the Canaries in the second tier, but he couldn’t find that magic touch to produce an upset.

Daniel Farke’s men thought they had the lead just before the 20 minute mark but Lukas Nmecha’s effort was ruled out by VAR after it spotted a handball in the build-up.



But just over 10 minutes later they grabbed the opener and there was no doubt at all over it after a stunner from Sean Longstaff.

It was a classy touch, turn and finish from the midfielder as he fired his effort beyond Daniel Grimshaw in the visiting goal.

11 minutes later, it was two.

Gabriel Gudmundsson reacted quickest in the box to a loose ball and slammed home to spark celebrations in the stands and on the park.



With five minutes left on the clock, they put the tie out of sight as Joel Piroe curled the ball home superbly as Leeds turned defence into attack in quick time.

Leeds had further chances to increase the gap further, while the visitors had an opportunity for a consolation but neither could convert.

BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK

Victory means Leeds are now in the hat for the quarter-final draw as they take another step towards FA Cup glory.

Focus will now naturally shift back to the Premier League survival fight with Crystal Palace next on the agenda.

For Norwich and Clement, it is back to Championship duty on Wednesday night at Carrow Road against Sheffield United before another home game next weekend against Preston.

Football.title: Inside Neil Warnock’s crazy unpaid return to management as 77-year-old fumes at referee in first game back
Football.content:
NEIL WARNOCK is back in the dugout and back on the richter scale.

At 77, the man with more promotions than a supermarket shelf has answered a desperate SOS from Torquay United — the club he credits for saving his managerial career 33 years ago.





On Saturday, the veteran— who boasts a record EIGHT promotions as a manager over 46 years— took charge of his 1,628th match, another all-time high in the game.

And despite a bruising 3-0 defeat in the National League South at Ebbsfleet, the fire in his belly is still raging.

When Torquay midfielder Matt Worthington got sent off for a mistimed tackle on Jake Hessenthaler early in the second half, Warnock was fuming with ref Kai Morgan, claiming he was being influenced by Ebbsfleet.

“They’re running the f***ing game,” he yelled at the official so loudly the entire stadium heard.



His loyal long-standing assistant Ronnie Jepson joined him in the dugout and barked: “You don’t know the f***ing game ref.”

But even Warnock had to calm down the big man — saying: “Forget him, Ronnie — it’s not worth it.”

And later he said: “The ref let 10 challenges go earlier worse than that.”

Warnock took caretaker charge of last Tuesday’s 2-2 draw at Farnborough after the Gulls sacked highly-popular boss Paul Wotton.



BEST FREE BETS AND BETTING SIGN UP OFFERS

And he says he will only be in dugout for the two upcoming home games against Salisbury tomorrow and Chesham on Saturday before handing over the reins to a new manager.

Warnock scaled the game from winning his first promotion by taking Scarborough into the EFL in 1987 to winning promotion to the top flight with the likes of Notts County, Sheffield United, QPR and Cardiff.

And yet here he was managing a team in the sixth tier that has not won for seven games — losing five of them.

Why? Because Torquay pulls at his heart-strings for a brief stint he had there in 1993.

He told me: “After taking Notts County up two divisions to the top flight, I turned down Chelsea out of loyalty — but within six months I got the sack.



“It killed me at the time and was thinking of packing it all in.

“But Kevin Blackwell, who was my goalkeeper at Scarborough and had been with me at County, talked me into going to Torquay for their last 14 games.

“We had some good offers at the time — Derby was one of them.



“But my wife Sharron and I went down to Torquay and we enjoyed it so much.

“They were going to get relegated but we managed to keep them in the Football League by winning at Carlisle in the penultimate game.

“We had players like Darren Moore and Paul Trollope going through brick walls for me for £50 a week.

“It gave me that belief again. I enjoyed motivating them and keeping them up.”

And it was while down in Devon that he visited Cornwall with Sharron on his days off — and the couple fell in love with the place and bought their forever home.



Warnock went on to grab two promotions with Plymouth but when his career took him to all parts of the country they kept on their farmhouse and rented wherever they went.

After a brief stint at Aberdeen two year ago, Warnock said he was finally calling it a day — but was helping Torquay out as an advisor.

But he said: “Last week they decided to part company with Paul and they literally had no-one who could take the team.



“They wanted me to do it until the end of the season but had to turn it down because I had other commitments. So I told them I’d do it for a maximum of four games.

“The club has a shortlist that has been whittled down and I’m helping them in that process.

“I like this league and the clubs in it. It’s not like I think this is beneath me. I started all those years ago in non-league with Gainsborough and was at Burton and Scarborough.

“I’m not getting paid. For me it’s nice to give something back and help a club who got me on the right path again.

“Torquay is like a throwback to the old-days. You don’t see Man United and Liverpool scarves down there. It’s all yellow and navy blue. We get crowds of between 3,000-5,000 — which is incredible for a team in the sixth tier.



“Although I’ve enjoyed it, I don’t like the fact we only had THREE outfield players on the bench and have an horrendous injury list — all key players.

“But we think some of them will be back next week and hopefully the club can still get a play-off spot.”

The Gulls currently sit sixth in the table and need to finish in the top seven to qualify. However, eight-placed Chelmsford are only two points behind with three games in hand.



Old school managers like Warnock seem to be going out of vogue.

But when you see a club like Tottenham in serious peril, you do wonder whether they should pick up phone to him.

Often teams in these situations just need someone who can generate a good changing room environment — his trademark.

Warnock said: “It wasn’t Thomas Frank’s fault. You can tell that the dressing room’s not good. I wouldn’t be surprised if it’s toxic in there.

“And you can’t do anything without a dressing room.



“I don’t know how somebody from abroad like the lad Igor Tudor who’s doing it, I don’t know how they’re going to get that changed, if I’m honest.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

“But they’ll have to do it, or they’ll be in the Championship next year, won’t they?”

If that happens the hierarchy at N17 could do a lost worse than lure him Warnock out of retirement AGAIN.

Football.title: Man Utd ‘draw up stunning five-man shortlist to replace Marcus Rashford including PSG ace and Premier League rival’
Football.content:
MARCUS RASHFORD looks to have played his last game for Manchester United after a positive season on loan at Barcelona.

The England star was cast aside at Old Trafford following a fall-out with Ruben Amorim last year.



He has scored ten goals and provided nine assists in all competitions and Barca are now “convinced” about triggering their £25million option to buy, according to Sport.



The Spanish outlet now believes there are five players United could look at to replace Rashford next season.

Bradley Barcola

Being part of PSG’s Champions League-winning squad has done Barcola the world of good and he’s thriving under Luis Enrique.

The 23-year-old has ten goals and four assists overall and continues to earn a reputation as one of football’s rising talents.

He has 10 goals in Ligue 1 this season, with four assists in all competitions.

Sport calls him “one of the most promising wingers in European football” and “the most high profile” name on United’s shortlist.

Yan Diomande

The 19-year-old RB Leipzig star is enjoying a terrific individual season on the wing.



He has 11 goals and five assists in all competitions from 21 starts and is turning heads among Europe’s elite.

The Ivory Coast international is said to have impressed United chiefs with his “speed and dribbling ability”.

Jesus Rodriguez

United are also “keen” on Como star Rodriguez, the 20-year-old Spaniard who is going from strength to strength under Cesc Fabregas.



Rodriguez has 15 starts in Serie A this term and repaid his manager’s faith with seven assists.

In all competitions, he has three goals after scoring and assisting in Como’s 3-1 win over Lecce at the end of last month.

Martial Godo

United have already placed their trust in one young talent called Martial in years gone by.

And there could be a new exciting prospect in France with 22-year-old Strasbourg star Godo generating interest from United.



The forward has 11 goals and one assist in all competitions having been signed by Liam Rosenior before he left for Chelsea.

The English-born star was previously on the books at Fulham and plays for Ivory Coast internationally.

Iliman Ndiaye

It’s not been the most prolific season at Everton for Ndiaye but Sport claim he has done enough to gain attention from the Red Devils.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

The Senegal international has six goals and three assists in all competitions for the Toffees after becoming a key man for David Moyes this season.



He has started 23 of Everton’s 29 Premier League games and is valued at around £40m by Transfermarkt.

Football.title: Celtic stars show true colours after X-rated Rangers fan abuse amid pitch riot and arrests
Football.content:
Celtic stars show true colours after X-rated Rangers fan abuse amid pitch riot and arrests

Celtic players and staff were subjected to foul-mouthed abuse from Rangers supporters as they left the pitch at Ibrox following their Scottish Cup quarter-final penalty shootout victory

View 2 Images Celtic's players could not hold back their emotions any longer (Image: Daily Record)

Celtic players and staff were subjected to explicit abuse following their victory over Rangers amidst a pitch invasion that sparked chaos at Ibrox. Several Parkhead individuals, including Julian Araujo, Tomas Cvancara and Shaun Maloney, encountered vulgar shouts from fans situated in the home end as they made their way up the tunnel.

‌



Daizen Maeda also let out a primal roar towards aggrieved Rangers supporters after receiving a barrage of criticism.

‌



This took place after stewards and police officers, trying to control the ongoing clash between supporters from both sides, guided the stars off the pitch. And it was the Celtic stars who had nowhere to hide as they were bombarded with shouts from irate punters after their side were knocked out of the Scottish Cup by their fiercest rivals on penalties.

‌



Celtic star speaks out on Rangers carnage in blood-stained interview READ MORE :

A video widely shared on social media depicts Hoops stars and coaching fans being vehemently berated by Rangers fans who had stayed behind to express their anger.

Police and football authorities now face a significant headache after the reintroduction of the full allocation of away fans to this fixture in six years ended in bitterness and rage, with some arrests being made in the aftermath of the ugly scenes.

‌



View 2 Images Police officer and stewards force fans back (Image: PA)

Speaking in his post-match press conference, Celtic boss O’Neill gave his thoughts. He said: “It was a big derby game. I think there was a lot of jubilation at us winning.

“Obviously the fans spilt over. I suppose it’s very difficult to quell that if that’s the case. Maybe it should be the first question, maybe it shouldn’t be. I thought it might be your third question.

‌



"I just thought the team were absolutely fantastic. Fourth game in ten days. Absolutely out on our feet. We defended magnificently for long periods of the game. Thankfully we came through.”

Asked if his staff and players were safe, he added: “As we were making our way back, there was some sort of fracass. Somebody tried to get on the field. I don’t know, all I saw was there was a bit of a scrum down at the bottom.

‌



"As you say, maybe it does taint proceedings. I think there was an element of self protection in that one, if that’s the case. I’m making judgments here on things that I only half saw.

Football.title: Ex-Man Utd cult hero speaks out as star left with sickening injury amid THREE red cards
Football.content:
Ex-Man Utd cult hero speaks out as star left with sickening injury amid THREE red cards

Antonio Valencia appears to be regularly keeping his eye on Ecuadorian football following his decision to retire as a player and he has now sent a message to one of his compatriots

View 2 Images Vernaza was rushed to hospital in an ambulance (Image: Getty Images)

Antonio Valencia has sent a touching message to one of his Ecuadorian countrymen after he suffered a horror leg-break mid-match. Jean Pierre Arroyo Vernaza was in action for Independiente del Valle on Saturday against top-flight rivals Mushuc Runa.

‌



But his match lasted just an hour. As he attempted to run through on goal, he was taken down by opposition defender Brian Negro.

‌



Vernaza was left with a clear ankle injury, with his foot facing the wrong way, while he fell onto the floor and screamed in pain. Negro was shown a red card for the tackle but protested his innocence, with a mass brawl then breaking out between the two sides, with others left in tears.

‌



Vernaza in the meantime received lengthy treatment from doctors before he was rushed to hospital in an ambulance. Former Manchester United star Valencia is among those to have offered his well-wishes following the incident, having suffered a similar injury during his time at Old Trafford.

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

Masked fan unplugs VAR during match as club release statement READ MORE :

Rangers and Celtic fans clash with police and staff on pitch in disgraceful Old Firm scenes READ MORE :

Taking to Twitter, he wrote: “Stay strong in these tough times, you have to come back even stronger. There is no other option, friend.”

‌



Meanwhile, a statement from Independiente del Valle read: “Club Independiente del Valle, in light of the unfortunate injury suffered by our player Jean Pierre Arroyo Vernaza, deeply appreciates the overwhelming number of messages, expressions of affection, and concern shown by fans, clubs, and the entire football community.

“This support and solidarity are a fundamental driving force for Jean Pierre to face this moment with strength and return to the pitch stronger than ever. The club sends him all our strength and wishes him a speedy and successful recovery. The entire Independiente del Valle team and family are with him during this difficult time.”

View 2 Images Jean Pierre Vernaza was left with a horror ankle injury (Image: Getty Images)

‌



Elsewhere, Negro has issued an apology in the aftermath. Speaking to Diario Ecuador, he said: "I apologize. Being faster, I caught his ankle. As a person and a footballer, I feel terrible. I've called to go to the hospital when things calm down, because it's still hot.

"I hadn't realized (what happened). Wishing him a successful operation and a speedy recovery.”

Independiente del Valle eventually went on to win 3-1. Elvis Velasco had given Mushuc Runa the lead, with Emerson Pata equalising before half-time.

Article continues below

Negro was sent off for his challenge on 63 minutes, with both Cristopher Angulo and Jordy Alcivar sent off following the brawl between the two sides. Independiente eventually secured victory with two late goals from Matias Mijail Perello.

Football.title: UK Government responds to 'sickening' Grok posts mocking Hillsborough and Munich
Football.content:
UK Government responds to 'sickening' Grok posts mocking Hillsborough and Munich

Liverpool and Manchester United have been targeted by vile posts on social media outlet X mocking the Hillsborough and Munich disasters, as well as the heart-breaking death of Diogo Jota

View 2 Images Posts generated by Grok have been slammed by the UK Government (Image: Getty Images)

The UK Government has slammed AI-generated posts mocking the Hillsborough and Munich disasters as 'sickening and irresponsible' and going against 'British values'. Users of the social media platform X have used their in-built AI tool Grok to construct the posts, which have now been deleted.

‌



As well as both disasters, the heart-breaking death of former Liverpool forward Diogo Jota had also been a target. The Mirror has seen the vile comments but have decided not to publish them in full given the nature of the contents.

‌



The UK Government has now spoken out and condemned the posts. In a statement shared by the BBC, the Department for Science, Innovation and Technology said: "These posts are sickening and irresponsible. They go against British values and decency.

‌



"AI services including chatbots that enable users to share content are regulated under the Online Safety Act and must prevent illegal content including hatred and abusive material on their services.

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

Celtic boss Martin O'Neill responds to Rangers pitch invasion that forced police to step in READ MORE :

"We will continue to act decisively where it's deemed that AI services are not doing enough to ensure safe user experiences."

‌



A spokesperson for UK watchdog Ofcom added: "Under the Online Safety Act, tech firms must assess the risk of people in the UK encountering illegal content on their platforms, take appropriate steps to reduce the risk of UK users encountering it, and take it down quickly when they become aware of it. Those companies that do not comply can expect to face enforcement action."

The statements from both the Government and Ofcom come after action from both Premier League sides in attempting to get the posts taken down. Both xAI, an American artificial intelligence company, and X are owned by Elon Musk, who completed a takeover of the social media platform in 2022.

View 2 Images The death of Diogo Jota was also mocked by the awful posts (Image: Getty Images)

‌



It is not the first time that X and Grok have come under scrutiny over recent months. They were both under the microscope earlier this year when the tool was used to undress people in images shared on social media.

Ofcom subsequently launched an investigation into xAI, who announced in January they had "implemented technological measures" to prevent the tool from doing so moving forward.

The latest controversy revolves around users prompting the AI tool to generate posts about both the Munich and Hillsborough disasters. Another user asked Grok to "vulgarly roast" Jota, with the subsequent post having been viewed over two million times before it was deleted.

Football.title: Celtic star speaks out on Rangers carnage in blood-stained interview
Football.content:
Celtic star speaks out on Rangers carnage in blood-stained interview

Tomas Cvancara came off the bench to score the decisive penalty as Celtic defeated Rangers in a dramatic Scottish Cup quarter-final at Ibrox

View 2 Images Celtic striker Tomáš Čvančara speaks on the win over Rangers (Image: Premier Sports)

Shoot-out hero Tomas Cvancara brushed aside chaotic pitch disturbances after guiding Celtic to victory over Rangers - despite blood visible on his shirt. The Borussia Monchengladbach loanee made a crucial impact on derby day after entering as a substitute to trouble the Rangers' backline in the Scottish Cup thriller.

‌



The towering forward dispatched his penalty past Jack Butland following extra-time to secure a semi-final berth for the Hoops.

‌



After the decisive moment, unsavoury scenes unfolded at Ibrox as supporters flooded onto the pitch. Police stepped in when fans from the home section moved towards the Celtic supporters in retaliation.

‌



Port Vale hero Ben Waine can't hide true feelings after huge Sunderland FA Cup shock READ MORE :

There were allegations that a member of Celtic's coaching staff had been assaulted during the disorder. Cvancara was spotted getting involved in an on-field incident.

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

Speaking to Premier Sports after the match, Cvancara appeared with blood visibly marking his kit alongside goalkeeper Viljami Sinisalo.

‌



Content cannot be displayed without consent

Questioned about the marks on his top by Premier Sports, the striker chose to concentrate on the outcome. He said: "It was a tough game. We know we have quality.

View 2 Images An incident is broken up on the pitch (Image: Premier Sports)

‌



"I trusted that everyone that was on the penalty was going to score and it was quite easy."

FREE William Hill Shop Bets inside your Mirror every day of Cheltenham Don't miss a FREE shop bet inside your favourite racing paper every day of the Cheltenham festival! Plus, get expert inside from our top tipsters with pullouts every day from Tuesday March 10 - Friday March 13, 2026 covering the runners, riders, and everything you need to make the 102nd Cheltenham festival the biggest ever. Pushed on the tension of the spot kick, he added: "It was a lot of pressure. We were on the opposite side of the pitch where the fans of Rangers [were].

Article continues below

"It was a lot of pressure and I just knew what I had to do."

Football.title: Gareth Bale gives Zinedine Zidane insight after Real Madrid icon linked to Man Utd job
Football.content:
Gareth Bale gives Zinedine Zidane insight after Real Madrid icon linked to Man Utd job

Gareth Bale has given an insight into Zinedine Zidane's coaching methods at Real Madrid as the Frenchman continues to be linked with the Manchester United manager's job

View 2 Images Gareth Bale worked with Zinedine Zidane at Real Madrid. (Image: Soccrates Images, Getty Images)

Former Real Madrid star Gareth Bale has offered a glimpse into Zinedine Zidane's coaching approach amid ongoing speculation linking him with Manchester United. Several potential contenders have been linked with the position at Old Trafford, with Michael Carrick in interim control until the season's end.

‌



Carrick was brought in during January on a temporary arrangement, but may be considered for the role permanently if he maintains his remarkable run of results for United. Prior to United's loss to Newcastle earlier this week, the team had secured six victories in seven fixtures, collecting 19 points from a potential 21 available in the Premier League, which has seen the club rise to third position in the standings.

‌



Despite the positive sequence, various contenders remain connected with the United manager's position permanently from the summer, with Zidane referenced regularly throughout recent years.

‌



Man Utd's unsung hero who has helped Michael Carrick in area he and Ruben Amorim differ on READ MORE :

FOLLOW OUR MAN UTD FB PAGE! Latest United news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Zidane has been without employment since departing Real Madrid in 2021, and has been consistently associated with a comeback to management ever since, with United and the France national side mentioned as prospective destinations.

Bale featured under Zidane throughout his time with Los Blancos, securing five Champions League crowns, three of which came under Zidane's stewardship. Discussing Zidane's tenure as boss at the Santiago Bernabeu, Bale offered a glimpse into what it was like working under the 53-year-old, especially during training sessions.

‌



"Zidane as a coach? Honestly, he didn't do much," Bale said on the Stick to Football podcast.

"We'd go over some tactics before matches against Barcelona or Bayern-15 minutes of defensive work. Other than that, it was just possession games, shooting drills, and then we'd go home.

View 2 Images Gareth Bale has given an insight into what it is like to work with Zinedine Zidane. (Image: NurPhoto, NurPhoto via Getty Images)

‌



"But he earned respect thanks to his qualities as a player. He participated in training, even beat me a few times – he's still in great shape."

United will resume Premier League duties next weekend when they host Aston Villa at Old Trafford, having enjoyed a break this week owing to their absence from the FA Cup.

The Reds will then travel to Bournemouth on March 22, their final fixture before the international break, and won't feature again until April 13 when they host rivals Leeds United at Old Trafford.

Football.title: Celtic boss Martin O'Neill responds to Rangers pitch invasion that forced police to step in
Football.content:
Celtic boss Martin O'Neill responds to Rangers pitch invasion that forced police to step in

Celtic’s Scottish Cup victory against fierce rivals Rangers was marred by chaotic scenes as fans flooded the pitch shortly after the final whistle with both managers having now spoken out

View 2 Images Ugly scenes marred Celtic's Scottish Cup win vs Rangers (Image: Steve Welsh/PA Wire)

Martin O’Neill admitted his concern that an angry pitch invasion could potentially taint Celtic’s Scottish Cup victory against Rangers, while rival boss Danny Rohl condemned the scenes.

‌



The Hoops beat their Old Firm rivals on penalties on Sunday afternoon. The game signalled the first occasion in recent seasons that Celtic were permitted to have their full complement of away fans at Ibrox - but things unfortunately turned ugly at the final whistle.

‌



Some Celtic fans spilled onto the pitch, with Rangers supporters responding in kind. They made their way over to rival fans, with missiles including pyrotechnics clearly thrown.

‌



Speaking in his post-match press conference, Celtic boss O’Neill gave his thoughts.. He said: “It was a big derby game. I think there was a lot of jubilation at us winning.

“Obviously the fans spilt over. I suppose it’s very difficult to quell that if that’s the case. Maybe it should be the first question, maybe it shouldn’t be. I thought it might be your third question.

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

‌



Rangers fans storm onto pitch after Celtic penalty shootout in chaotic Old Firm scenes READ MORE :

Football fans clash in violent scenes as police forced to use tasers in huge brawl READ MORE :

"I just thought the team were absolutely fantastic. Fourth game in ten days. Absolutely out on our feet. We defended magnificently for long periods of the game. Thankfully we came through.”

Asked if his staff and players were safe, he added: “As we were making our way back, there was some sort of fracass. Somebody tried to get on the field. I don’t know, all I saw was there was a bit of a scrum down at the bottom.

"As you say, maybe it does taint proceedings. I think there was an element of self protection in that one, if that’s the case. I’m making judgments here on things that I only half saw.

‌



“From my viewpoint, it was a great, great win for us. We were under pressure during the course of the game, as you would expect Rangers are very strong. They’ve had the full week to go through. We’ve had to travel to Aberdeen, our fourth away game in ten days. Just fantastic effort by the players, really, really fantastic.”

View 2 Images Rangers Ultras invade the pitch following the Scottish Gas Scottish Cup Quarter Final match between Rangers and Celtic (Image: Ian MacNicol/Getty Images)

O’Neill then said: “I think that would be a worry [tainted the game]. For me, from a distance, I’ve been saying that the fixture itself, although still a phenomenal fixture there’s no question about that, lost a bit of its lustre because of the away fans, the full compliment of away fans weren’t allowed in.

‌



“I have no idea what might happen. From that viewpoint yes. I think today, with our crowd in there, to me it was really a proper Old Firm game in that sense. We’ll see what develops.”

Rangers boss Danny Rohl has also spoken out on the scenes. He said: “I’m honest, I was not on the pitch in this moment. I didn’t see it until now. Just that something was on the pitch which was the case.

“I think we all know all of the emotion of the situation after a game. Nobody likes to see this. I think it was a great atmosphere for 120 minutes. I think it should be like this. All the other things should not be in the stadium or in football.”

Football.title: Declan Rice reveals what sets Arsenal apart from Premier League title rivals
Football.content:
Declan Rice reveals what sets Arsenal apart from Premier League title rivals

Arsenal are currently the favourites to claim this year’s Premier League title under Mikel Arteta which would see them finally secure the trophy after years of near-misses

View 2 Images Declan Rice has shared his verdict on Arsenal's title chances (Image: Getty Images)

Declan Rice claims that Arsenal’s lack of long-term injuries has seen them move ahead of their rivals in the race for the Premier League title. The Gunners are currently in prime position to lift the trophy come the end of the season.

‌



Arsenal sit seven points clear of nearest rivals Manchester City with eight games to be played. Should they go all the way, it would see the club end their over two-decade wait for a league title, while it would see them finally get over the line having been pipped in each of the last two campaigns.

‌



Midfielder Rice has suggested that Arsenal’s lack of injuries has seen them put themselves in control. Speaking to Hayters, he said: “Last year, the injuries killed us.

‌



"I know a lot of teams this year have been having the same problem, so I can't complain about that, but we lost a lot of key players last year. We have an amazing squad this year. Players you can rely on in every position.

FOLLOW OUR ARSENAL FB PAGE! Latest Gunners news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Arsenal suffer new injury blow as attacker forced off vs Mansfield in FA Cup battle READ MORE :

“Also, there’s a calmness. You go in every day, you see the boys. It’s not all serious. It’s calm, it’s relaxed.

‌



"It's like a family. You're going in, everyone’s in their early 20s, no one’s really above 30. Everyone's vibing. It's a nice group to be around.

"We have such a good set of staff all around the building, every department. So everything’s been really good from start to start to now. We’re getting into the business end, which is the most important part now."

Arsenal have of course had to deal with a few issues this season; Kai Havertz and Martin Odegaard, Mikel Merino and William Saliba all among those to have had lengthy periods on the sidelines.

‌



View 2 Images Rice and Gabriel have been key to Arsenal this season (Image: Getty Images)

And that saw boss Mikel Arteta make a number of changes to his side for the FA Cup fifth round clash with Mansfield Town.

"Well, they had issues and they've been carrying issues," Arteta said when asked to explain his selection decisions. "And the amount of games that we are playing, it's obviously super demanding.

‌



"It was the only time to try to make sure that we can clean those issues that they're having to start the next sequence of two weeks before the break in the best possible way. And we had to make those decisions."

Arsenal turn their attention to the Champions League on Wednesday night with a clash with German side Bayer Leverkusen. They will then be aiming to continue their push for the title as they take on Everton.

Football.title: Masked fan unplugs VAR during match as club release statement
Football.content:
Masked fan unplugs VAR during match as club release statement

One fan took it into their own hands to try and end the use of VAR during a football match in Germany and they certainly caused an interruption through their masked escapades

View 2 Images The VAR screen was not working (Image: Sky)

A masked vigilante fan unplugged a VAR screen in protest of the technology's use during a match in Germany. When referee Felix Bickel was called to the sidelines to review a penalty decision during Preussen Munster vs Hertha Berlin, he was unable to see a replay.

‌



After initially not awarding a spot-kick when Munster's Niko Koulis brought down Michael Cuisance, Bickel was advised to his VAR to take another look, but couldn't. A Munster ultra had unplugged the monitor in a bid to sabotage the use of technology.

‌



The action lined up with a banner in the stands that read: "Pull the plug on VAR." The saboteur was not spotted in action, but a supporter wearing a green morph-like face covering and overalls that would not go amiss at a crime scene investigation was seen being helped back into the stands.

‌



For opponents of VAR, it was bad news, however, as the technology was still able to be used. Video referee Katrin Rafalski ultimately took the decision from a remote location, in the VAR room in Cologne.

The club home side have said they are investigating the incidents and are putting actions in place to avoid a repeat of the situation.

Rangers fans storm onto pitch after Celtic penalty shootout in chaotic Old Firm scenes READ MORE :

Newcastle fan rubs salt into wounds with Alan Shearer tribute after Sunderland goal READ MORE :

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

‌



A statement from Preussen Munster read: "In first-half stoppage time between SC Preußen Münster and Hertha BSC, VAR intervened after a controversial incident in the penalty area.

However, the referee was unable to review the incident using the VAR monitor in the stadium because a masked spectator from the active fan section illegally entered the pitch and unplugged the technical equipment.

"The penalty decision was ultimately made by video referee Katrin Rafalski in the VAR room in Cologne.

‌



View 2 Images Fans of SC Preussen Münster wave flags during the 2. Bundesliga match between SC Preußen Münster and Hertha BSC at Preussenstadion (Image: Christof Koepsel/Getty Images)

"SC Preußen Münster regrets the incident and will do everything in its power to identify and bring the perpetrator(s) to justice. Furthermore, immediate measures have been taken to prevent similar incidents in the future.

"Initial findings indicate that this was a planned action – a banner to that effect was displayed in the home section shortly after the technical malfunction."

Article continues below

Munster ultimately lost the Bundesliga 2 match 2-1 after Hertha netted a 93rd-minute goal through Martin Winkler. The away side had taken the lead via the penalty in the opening half as Fabian Reese scored from the spot but Jannis Heuer found an equaliser in the second period.

Football.title: Boro thrash QPR to boost automatic promotion hopes
Football.content:
Middlesbrough emphatically boosted their automatic promotion hopes by thrashing QPR 4-0 at Loftus Road.

David Strelec put Boro ahead before Alan Browne and Hayden Hackney added second-half goals, and Tommy Conway's 88th-minute penalty completed a resounding victory.

It moved second-placed Middlesbrough four points clear of Millwall in third, and five behind Championship leaders Coventry with 10 matches of the season remaining.

They went ahead in the 20th minute with a fortuitous goal that arguably should not have stood.

Riley McGree collected Hackney's clever pass and played a low ball in from the left towards Morgan Whittaker. The striker's first-time poke was off-target but ricocheted back to him off R's defender Rhys Norrington-Davies, and his second attempt hit the elbow of Strelec, who was standing near the goal-line, and went in.

Isaac Hayden had a header saved by Boro 'keeper Sol Brynn, but the visitors remained on top and went close to doubling their lead before the interval when Hackney sent an effort wide of the far post, and when Hackney's corner was flicked over the bar by Luke Ayling.

Boro continued to threaten after the restart, and 'keeper Joe Walsh denied Strelec at point-blank range shortly before Matt Targett volleyed wide of Walsh's right-hand post.

Image: Hayden Hackney celebrates after scoring Middlesbrough's third goal at Loftus Road

Rangers did then manage to put them under a spell of pressure, but the second goal arrived in the 68th minute.

After Jimmy Dunne's poor attempt to head Hackney's corner clear, Ayling's looping effort came back off the bar and Brown got in front of QPR striker Richard Kone to tuck away the loose ball.

The outstanding Hackney made it three with a superb goal four minutes later.

Conway played the ball in from the left and McGree's clever dummy allowed it to run to Hackney, who steered a fine strike beyond Walsh and into the far corner.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Take a look at Hackney's 'outstanding' finish here

And Conway added a late fourth from the penalty spot after being bundled over in the box by young defender Tylon Smith, who had just come on for his Championship debut.

While Middlesbrough remain on course for the Premier League despite a strong challenge from in-form Millwall, Rangers' promotion chances have evaporated during a dismal recent spell.

They were in the thick of the race for the play-offs before losing their past three matches, failing to score a single goal and conceding 11 in the process - although they very nearly scored late on when Harvey Vale's curling shot hit a post.

The managers

QPR head coach Julien Stephan admitted his struggling team had simply been beaten by a better side:

"When you lose against a top, top team, you have to accept [it]. And they are better than us. This is the reality. They deserve their win," he said.

"They had more quality, individually and collectively. More quality with the ball and without the ball. I have to say that we lost against a better team than us. We will not face an opponent like this every time, but we need to be able to show more quality in possession.

"In the last three games we didn't score. In football if you don't score and are only in defensive positions it's very difficult to get a good result.

"Of course we are not happy. It's a difficult moment when you lose three games in a row and you concede many goals. But I want to protect my players, because I know them and I know they tried to give their best. We need to stay together."

Middlesbrough boss Kim Hellberg hailed Hayden Hackney and Luke Ayling after they played key roles in the victory at QPR:

"Hayden's a top player. He has everything and will end up being in the Premier League some day, hopefully with us," said Hellberg.

"The quality he has as a player is unbelievable - the way he drives with the ball and he is also working on his finishing.

"He's unbelievable to work with - such a pleasure and just a great guy for us to work with."

On Ayling, he added: "He was absolutely brilliant and has been in the last two games. So strong from him - how he plays on the ball, how he defends, two top, top performances from Luke.

"He's a big leader for us, together with a lot of the players. It's a very strong group with a lot of players that are setting an example every day at the training ground.

"With his experience and also his energy - he's a very energetic person - he does everything at the training ground to get better, and that's one of the things I like so much about him."

Football.title: Sivo hat-trick takes Leeds to Castleford victory as Hull KR bounce back
Football.content:
Maika Sivo scored a hat-trick as Leeds claimed a 22-4 Betfred Super League win over Castleford.

The winger's impressive contribution took his tally to nine tries in three games and secured three successive Super League wins for the Rhinos.

Castleford fell behind at Headingley when George Brown opened the scoring with his first Super League try and Jake Connor converted.

The visitors then replied when Darnell McIntosh quickly offloaded for Mikaele Ravalawa to cross in the corner, but Tom Weaver's subsequent conversion attempt flew wide.

Image: Sivo celebrates his second try against Castleford

Sivo opened his account in the 29th minute. Leeds claimed a 10-4 lead at half-time and Sivo extended their advantage further early in the second half from the wing, with Connor converting.

Rhinos charged ahead when Sivo scored his third of the game in the 55th minute, with Connor's kick also successful.

Leeds then saw out the win to climb into second in the table, two points behind leaders Wigan, while the Tigers sit in 10th.

Hull KR shrug off Vegas hangover

Hull KR shrugged off their Las Vegas nightmare and belatedly kick-started the defence of their Betfred Super League title with a 32-6 win at hapless Huddersfield.

Rovers were half a world away from last season's all-conquering treble-winning campaign when they crashed to a 58-6 loss to Leeds at the Allegiant Stadium last week.

They were effectively gifted their first points of the domestic year as Tom Davies and Mikey Lewis both crossed twice in a six-try show against the rock-bottom Giants.

Oliver Gildart served an ominous warning by scoring Rovers' opener with less than three minutes on the clock, and although the expected avalanche was not forthcoming, the visitors barely had to break sweat as they proceeded to wrap up victory.

Image: George Flanagan of Huddersfield fails to stop Tom Davies of Hull KR from scoring a try (Photo: Olly Hassell/SWpix.com)

Good spells of possession from the Giants, who added Sam Halsall and Zac Woolford to their injury list last week, were undermined by frequent errors and an inspired piece of playmaking as Tyrone May sent Davies over for Rovers' second on 28 minutes.

May was proving the game's star performer by some distance, and he served up Rovers' third in the 38th minute with a brilliant, looping ball to send Joe Burgess in on the left flank.

One conversion apiece from Lewis and Rhyse Martin bolstered Rovers' half-time advantage to 16 points, and they increased it within moments of the restart as Lewis made space and threw an outrageous dummy to wrongfoot Asher O'Donnell and trot over for his side's fourth.

Huddersfield broke through when the gutsy George Flanagan dropped onto Tui Lolohea's high kick under the posts. But Rovers responded with a sweeping, crossfield move that sent Davies in for his second.

May sent Lewis clattering over before Martin contributed his third and final successful kick.

Sky Sports will again show every game of the Super League live this season - including two matches in each round exclusively live, with the remaining five matches each week shown on Sky Sports+

Football.title: New Zealand's hearts broken again as India add to white-ball dynasty
Football.content:
"I wouldn't mind breaking a few hearts," said New Zealand captain Mitchell Santner on the eve of the T20 World Cup final against India in their opponents' homeland.

In the end, though, the only hearts that were broken were New Zealand's. Again.

Four World Cup finals for the Black Caps now and four defeats. Two in the 50-over arena, two in T20. They are cricket's nearly men.

A 96-tun trouncing in Ahmedabad to an all-conquering India may not be quite as galling as the "barest of margins" loss to England in a bonkers 2019 one-day international showpiece at Lord's.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player India lift the T20 World Cup trophy once again after successfully defending the title they won in the Caribbean in 2024

On that occasion, there was a cigarette paper between the teams. If Santner had run a bye off the final delivery of the Kiwis' innings it could have been them lifting the trophy.

On Sunday, however, there was a chasm between the sides. India looted 255 as New Zealand were punished for wayward bowling and bizarre plans. The Black Caps were bundled out for 159 in reply.

They were pretty much out of the game after India sprinted to 98-0 in seven overs. At 203-1, after 15, they looked toast.

World Cup glory eludes New Zealand again

Like all good underdogs, New Zealand are capable of winning games they are not expected to. Few backed them to overcome an unbeaten South Africa in the semi-finals but they pulverised them, off the back of Finn Allen's T20 World Cup-record 33-ball ton.

But they have not been able to win games when global silverware is on the line since they beat India in the Champions Trophy final way back in 2000, at least not in white-ball cricket.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Abhishek Sharma took the trophy-sealing wicket for India as New Zealand's Jacob Duffy ballooned into the deep

They did upset India to claim the inaugural World Test Championship mace in Southampton in 2021 - the same side they swept 3-0 in late 2024 to inflict a first Test series defeat on their rivals since England achieved the feat 12 years earlier.

The Black Caps need to channel that, and their latest white-ball hurt, into the World Cups ahead, the 2027 50-over version in Africa and 2028 T20 edition which will be held across New Zealand and Australia. There is every reason to believe they can contend in those.

A few players are in their mid-thirties now, including Santner, key seamers Matt Henry and Lockie Ferguson, and batter Daryl Mitchell but Allen and Rachin Ravindra, both 26, and fellow batter Glenn Phillips, 29 - who is also a gun fielder - are here for the long haul.

The problem they face, the problem everyone faces, is India, whose third straight victory in an ICC event - 2024 T20 World Cup, 2025 Champions Trophy and now 2026 T20 World Cup - secured their spot as the preeminent white-ball side of the times.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Sky Sports pundits Nasser Hussain and Michael Atherton review India's T20 World Cup triumph and what went wrong for New Zealand at Narendra Modi Stadium

India have only lost one match across those three title wins, against South Africa earlier in the 2026 T20 World Cup.

Frightening depth and 'genius' Bumrah make India formidable

Change of personnel has not altered the results. Gone are Virat Kohli and Rohit Sharma in T20s - they remain embedded in the ODI format - and in have come Sanju Samson and Abhishek Sharma.

The former ended this T20 World Cup with 97 not out and two brutal knocks of 89. The latter rebounded from three ducks in a row to start the event with an 18-ball fifty in the final. Tilak Varma, who collared England's Jofra Archer in the semi-finals, is also a top talent.

India's batting depth is frightening.

It often boggles the mind that Yashasvi Jaiswal is not a fulcrum of the white-ball sides. He may become that in time.

Then there is 14-year-old IPL record-breaker Vaibhav Suryavanshi. Sooner or later, you sense he will step up. Others will emerge, too, as the IPL continues to go from strength to strength.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player India's 'genius' Jasprit Bumrah took four wickets in the T20 World Cup final - all with slower balls

Underpinning everything, though, is the bowling genius of Jasprit Bumrah, who unsurprisingly came to the fore in the final, dismissing four New Zealanders with slower balls to close out an emphatic victory after the batters had gone berserk earlier on.

Bumrah had removed Harry Brook in Thursday's semi-final with a pace-off ball and then conceded just six runs in one over and eight in another at the death, nailing his yorkers to pretty much extinguish England's hopes of making the final.

He is a cricketing magician.

Sky Sports' Nasser Hussain said: "India's formula is pretty simple.

"A batting line‑up full of powerful hitters that will get you an above‑par score and a bowler in Bumrah, who makes a below‑par score probably enough. He's an absolute genius, and when you combine those two elements, they're virtually unbeatable."

New Zealand once again won hearts but not the trophy. India once again broke hearts and won the trophy.

We could be seeing a lot more of the latter over the next few years. Quite the dynasty is building.

Football.title: Leeds power past Norwich to reach first FA Cup quarter-final since 2003
Football.content:
Leeds reached their first FA Cup quarter-final since 2003 after easing past Championship side Norwich with a 3-0 victory at Elland Road.

Following a day of FA Cup shocks with Southampton's win over Fulham and Port Vale's success against Sunderland, all eyes were on Elland Road to see whether Norwich could follow their EFL counterparts with a win at Leeds.

However, first-half goals from Sean Longstaff (32) and Gabriel Gudmundsson (43) put Daniel Farke's side in full control against the Leeds' boss' former side.

Norwich failed to register a shot on target in the opening 45 minutes and while they fared better in the second half, the game was made safe by Joel Piroe late on as Leeds reached the last eight for the first time in 23 years.

Leeds bounced back to winning ways after successive Premier League defeats left them three points above the relegation zone, while Norwich failed to reproduce the form that saw them win nine of their previous 11 matches in all competitions.

How Leeds cruised past Norwich...

Image: Gabriel Gudmundsson celebrates his goal with Jaka Bijol and Willy Gnonto

Farke, who twice led Norwich to Premier League promotion and reached the FA Cup last eight with them six years ago, watched from the stands as he served a one-game ban for his red card in last week's home defeat by Manchester City.

After a slow start, Lukas Nmecha turned home a loose ball from inside the penalty area, but the goal was ruled out after a VAR intervention for Willy Gnonto's handball as he challenged Norwich goalkeeper Daniel Grimshaw in the build-up.

However, the Premier League side eventually took a deserved lead in the 32nd minute. Gudmundsson charged on to Gnonto's pass into the area and pulled the ball back for Longstaff, who swivelled on to his excellent first touch to volley home his first goal since September.

Image: Longstaff shoots on the turn to fire Leeds in front

Norwich went close to a quick response through Ali Ahmed's low shot, but Leeds were soon back on the offensive when Gnonto's header was ruled out for offside.

The travelling fans will have been disappointed at their in-form side's passive first-half display and it got worse for the visitors when Leeds struck their second two minutes before the break. The Canaries failed to deal with another low cross from James and Gudmundsson pounced to lash home his first goal for the club from 10 yards.

Norwich lifted themselves early in the second period, with Liam Gibbs wayward from inside the area and Kenny McLean's low shot - their first effort on target - easily gathered by Lucas Perri.

Piroe and Norwich winger Ahmed both took on drinks to break their Ramadan fast in the 63rd minute when the visitors sent on substitutes Jack Stacey, Anis Ben Slimane and Errol Mundle-Smith.

Image: Joel Piroe scores Leeds' third goal against Norwich

Slimane then squandered a better chance for the visitors, firing into the side-netting at the far post, while Piroe's 20-yard shot hit the crossbar in the 71st minute.

Piroe added Leeds' third following a swift counter-attack, applying a neat finish after Nmecha and substitute Anton Stach had combined to create the opening as Leeds sealed a place in the last eight for the first time since 2003 under former manager Terry Venables.

Farke looking forward to 'important weeks' ahead

Leeds boss Daniel Farke to TNT Sport:

"We were the favourites today but you only have to look at what happened elsewhere. Teams around us in the [Premier League] table rotated today and went out.

"I can't complain at all. Important minutes in the tank for some players and able to rest others.

"Sometimes it is OK if you have two or three changes to a team with core bones. If you rotate in nine positions you need time. We deserved to be 2-0 up. Second half the game-management could at times have been better but, in general, we didn't allow many chances.

"It was an important win for us. A win is always good for the confidence. The spirit in the group, everyone keeps going. Today some players had the chance to shine and it is important for us as a group.

"Apart from us just seven other teams can win the FA Cup. It is maybe not realistic for us. We are on a good path and we are looking forward to our upcoming games and important weeks for us."

Clement: Part of Norwich's journey

Norwich City head coach Philippe Clement to TNT Sport:

"There were ups and downs. For a lot of the guys it was the first time they have played at this level.

"It was difficult to adapt to the intensity. In the second half we played much better, creating opportunities.

"It is a very young squad, three months ago they were playing relegation football in the Championship and I am convinced they will improve from this. It is part of the journey they have to take to get to this level where they want to be."

When is the FA Cup quarter-final draw?

The FA Cup quarter-final draw is on Monday and will take place before the final fifth-round tie between West Ham and Brentford.

The draw is expected to start at around 7.05pm.

Football.title: India hammer New Zealand in final to make T20 World Cup history
Football.content:
India became the first men's side to win the T20 World Cup three times and the first to claim back-to-back titles with an emphatic 96-run victory over New Zealand continuing their white-ball dominance.

India smoked 255-5 after losing the toss in Ahmedabad, with Sanju Samson (89 off 46) passing fifty for the third straight match and opening partner Abhishek Sharma (52 off 21) slamming the fastest fifty of this year's tournament, from 18 deliveries.

New Zealand were bundled out for 159 in 19 overs in reply - they were 72-5 after 8.1 - as pace spearhead Jasprit Bumrah (4-15) bagged each of his wickets with slower balls.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Watch the moment the last New Zealand wicket fell as India claimed back-to-back Men's T20 World Cup titles and a record third in total

Score summary - India beat New Zealand by 96 runs India 255-5 in 20 overs (put in to bat): Sanju Samson (89 off 46 balls), Ishan Kishan (54 off 25), Abhishek Sharma (52 off 21), Shivam Dube (26no off 8), Hardik Pandya (18 off 13); Jimmy Neesham (3-46) New Zealand 159 all out in 19 overs (target 256): Tim Seifert (52 off 26), Mitchell Santner (43 off 35); Jasprit Bumrah (4-15), Axar Patel (3-27)

Bumrah was on a hat-trick after cleaning up Jimmy Neesham (8) and Matt Henry (0) in the 16th over and ended with career-best T20I figures after dismissing New Zealand captain Mitchell Santner (43 off 35).

Ishan Kishan - who had earlier tonked 54 from 25 balls - also produced two excellent catches, the second of which, a juggling effort at deep midwicket, removed Tim Seifert (52 off 26), and then a simple grab to get rid of Daryl Mitchell (17).

Since going over a decade without picking up a global limited-overs title following Champions Trophy glory in England in 2013, India have won the last three, with their T20 triumphs in 2024 and now this year sandwiching the 2025 Champions Trophy.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player India's Sanju Samson smoked 89 from 46 balls as his side hammered New Zealand in the T20 World Cup final

India beat New Zealand in that 50-over final a year ago and routed the same side on Sunday, becoming the first men's team to win the T20 World Cup at home and extending the Black Caps' wait for a maiden World Cup title after a fourth defeat in a final.

India get off to a flier in final

An early onslaught left the Kiwis shellshocked as Samson and Abhishek pounded India to 98-0 after seven overs - ample wides adding to the score and New Zealand's decision to select pacer Jacob Duffy (0-42) in place of off-spinner Cole McConchie spectacularly backfiring - while the hosts were then 203-1 after 15.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Jimmy Neesham took three wickets in the 16th over of the final, removing Samson, Ishan Kishan and India skipper Suryakumar Yadav

Neesham's three-wicket burst in one over, in which Samson, Kishan and Suryakumar Yadav all fell - Suryakumar for a golden duck to a diving catch from Rachin Ravindra in the deep - dragged New Zealand back into the game, only for Shivam Dube (26no off 8) to collar Neesham's final over for 24 as India eclipsed the 253-7 they piled on against England in the semi-final run-fest in Mumbai.

Dube dropped Finn Allen on nought in the first over of the chase, a blunder that could have cost India with the Kiwi opener blazing a T20 World Cup record 33-ball hundred in the nine-wicket demolition of South Africa in the last four.

However, Allen (9) holed out off Axar (3-27) Patel before Bumrah removed Ravindra (1) with his first delivery - a superb slower ball that Kishan caught on the dive at deep square - and India went on claim a first victory over New Zealand in four attempts at T20 World Cups following losses in 2007, 2016 and 2021.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player 'Magician' Jasprit Bumrah dismissed Rachin Ravindra from the first ball he bowled in Ahmedabad

India will aim to claim a first 50-over World Cup since the 2011 edition on home soil when the ODI showpiece is next held in Africa towards the end of 2027, having finished runners-up to Australia in 2023.

India banish Ahmedabad blues

Since that defeat in Ahmedabad three years ago, India have only suffered one loss across the next three ICC limited-overs events - earlier this month against South Africa in the Super 8s, also in Ahmedabad.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Kishan's juggling catch removed New Zealand opener Tim Seifert for 52 off 26 deliveries

There would be no issues at that venue against New Zealand, though, with batting brutality, fielding excellence - bar Dube's early dose of butterfingers and some sloppy errors from Hardik Pandya - and genius Bumrah playing their part.

Bumrah's final wicket came when he castled Santner with a delicious off-cutter in the 18th over, before Abhishek picked up the title-winning wicket when Duffy (3) skied to Tilak Varma at long-on.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player New Zealand's Daryl Mitchell was unhappy after Arshdeep Singh's throw towards the non-striker's stumps made contact with him

New Zealand's runners-up finish follows the same result in the 2015 and 2019 50-over World Cups and 2021 T20 World Cup.

They lost by the "barest of margins", to nick Ian Smith's iconic phrase, against England in an ODI World Cup epic seven years ago at Lord's, but this defeat at Narendra Modi Stadium was gargantuan.

Football.title: Crowd violence overshadows Celtic's Old Firm shoot-out win in Scottish Cup
Football.content:
Crowd trouble marred Celtic's dramatic penalty shoot-out victory over Old Firm rivals Rangers which secured them a spot in the Scottish Cup semi-finals.

Fans of both clubs spilled on to the pitch at full-time and launched missiles at one another as the police were forced to break up the violence.

This was the first time travelling supporters had been allowed the traditional away allocation of 7,500 tickets in eight years.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Celtic boss Martin O'Neill reacts to crowd trouble following his side's dramatic penalty win against Old Firm rivals Rangers

Despite failing to register a shot on target in 120 minutes of goalless football, last year's beaten finalists Celtic found a way to win again. Rangers captain James Tavernier hit the bar with the first penalty before fellow substitute Djeidi Gassama blazed over, with Tomas Cvancara then sealing the victory as Celtic ran out 4-2 winners from the spot before dozens of their fans invaded the pitch.

Image: The Celtic squad celebrates as Tomas Cvancara scores the winning penalty

That sparked an invasion from hundreds of Rangers supporters and missiles were thrown as police and stewards moved to form a barrier. Flares were thrown and some minor skirmishes appeared to take place before order was restored and the away fans were left to celebrate alone.

With league leaders Hearts, cup holders Aberdeen and fellow top-six sides Motherwell and Hibernian already out, Martin O'Neill's Celtic are now red-hot favourites to lift the trophy. They will learn their last-four opponents following the conclusion of St Mirren vs Partick Thistle this evening [7.30pm].

Danny Röhl's Rangers are now out of both domestic cup competitions and Europe, meaning the league title is their only remaining hope of lifting silverware.

How Celtic booked their semi-final spot

The crowd trouble started well before kick-off as ticketless Celtic supporters forced entry to the Broomloan Stand, causing Police Scotland to close the turnstiles for a short period of time.

On the pitch, the match started in similar to fashion to last week's league clash at Ibrox with the home side well on top.

Celtic were missing captain Callum McGregor and Kieran Tierney following injuries picked up in the midweek win at Aberdeen.

Luke McCowan came into central midfield, while Liam Scales moved to left-back as Auston Trusty returned amid a three-match league suspension. Daizen Maeda started through the middle.

Image: Youssef Chermiti challenges Hyun-Jun Yang

There was also a notable omission in the Rangers team as John Souttar was dropped to the bench along with Tochi Chukwuani.

The hosts forced the early pressure and there was a flashpoint when Trusty blocked Youssef Chermiti's strike with an arm. But it was tucked into his body and video assistant Andrew Dallas did not summon referee Don Robertson to the monitor.

Rangers were well on top in the opening quarter but that was the only real scare Celtic had and the visitors grew into the game.

Image: Rangers' Emmanuel Fernandez scored before it was ruled out for a handball

The away fans were celebrating 10 minutes from half-time when Maeda netted with a diving header but Scales was ultimately ruled offside by VAR after heading on McCowan's cross. It was one of the few times that Celtic threatened in the match.

Dujon Sterling and Chermiti looped efforts over before the break and Rangers spent most of the second period in the Celtic half.

Hoops goalkeeper Viljami Sinisalo stood up well to the aerial pressure and came off his line quickly to foil Chermiti following a mistake from Benjamin Arthur. The visitors needed some last-ditch blocks from Trusty and Sebastian Tounekti in particular to keep the scores blank.

Image: Celtic's Tomas Cvancara scores the winning penalty against Rangers

Rangers' physical advantage was forcing Celtic back and there was little respite for the visitors, who ended the 90 minutes with an all-new front three of Joel Mvuka, Cvancara and James Forrest.

The pattern continued into extra-time. Substitute Dane Murray's block denied Chermiti and Rangers then had a goal disallowed by VAR after the ball went into the net off the hand of Emmanuel Fernandez following a corner.

Image: Rangers' James Tavernier (C) and team-mates watch on during the penalty shoot-out

Chermiti volleyed wide from a good chance and Sinisalo saved from Nedim Bajrami as the action continued to flow one way, with Rangers ending the 120 minutes with 24 shots to Celtic's one.

Rangers won the toss for the spot-kicks but could not take advantage in front of the Copland Road Stand.

Rangers captain Tavernier went up first, but his effort smacked the crossbar and flew over.

Goalkeeper Jack Butland, who had saved eight of his previous nine penalties couldn't get near any of Celtic's four expertly dispatched kicks.

Gassama also saw his effort go over the top as Cvancara kept his goal to win the shoot-out 4-2 and spark chaos inside Ibrox.

Celtic supporters came onto the pitch to celebrate the result before a counter charge came from the home support.

Police and security intervened as the players were escorted off the field of play while both sets of fans launched missiles and pyrotechnics at one another.

The ugly scenes have since been condemned by the Scottish FA, who have vowed to carry out an investigation in line with Judicial Panel Protocol.

O'Neill: It was a proper Old Firm game

Celtic boss Martin O'Neill speaking in his press conference on whether the pitch invasion overshadowed the win:

"That would be a worry for me. The fixture itself, although still a phenomenal fixture, had lost a bit of its lustre because the away fans or full complement of away fans were not allowed in.

"We have no idea what might happen. I think today, with the crowd and our crowd there, to me, it was a proper Old Firm game in that sense. We will see what develops.

Image: Celtic manager Martin O'Neill looks on against Rangers

"It's a big derby game and I think there was a lot of jubilation at us winning. The fans spilt over and I suppose it is very difficult to quell that, I don't know.

"As we were making our way back, there was some kind of fracas and someone had tried to get on the field. I don't know, all I saw was a bit of a scrum down at the bottom.

"As you say, maybe it does taint proceedings but I think there was an element of self-protection in that one. I am making judgements on things I only half saw. From my viewpoint, it was a great win for us. Rangers are very strong and had the full week to go at it."

Röhl: Nobody wants these scenes

Rangers boss Danny Röhl speaking in his press conference about the pitch invasion:

"I was not on the pitch in the moment, I did not see it until now.

Image: Rangers boss Danny Röhl looks dejected as his side are knocked out of the Scottish Cup
